title: Yara and the Shy Little Bunny
synopsis: When Yara finds a scared little bunny hiding in her garden, she learns that being gentle and patient can help make new friends. Through kindness and sharing, Yara shows the bunny that friendship can bloom anywhere.

pages.text: Yara loved playing in her colorful garden. She had curly black hair and wore her favorite yellow dress with tiny flowers on it. One morning, she saw something move behind the big sunflowers.

illustration.description: A cheerful girl with curly black hair and warm brown skin wearing a sunny yellow dress with small flower patterns. She stands in a vibrant garden with tall sunflowers, colorful vegetables, and green plants. Her expression is curious and happy. Bright cartoon style with bold primary colors and rounded shapes.
illustration.alt: Yara in her garden looking curious

pages.text: Behind the flowers was a tiny gray bunny. The bunny looked very scared and sad. Its little ears were flat against its head, and it was shaking. Yara felt worried for the little bunny.

illustration.description: A small gray bunny with droopy ears pressed against its head, looking frightened and trembling behind large yellow sunflowers. The bunny has big, sad eyes. Yara is visible in the background, her face showing concern and kindness. Soft green garden setting with textured paper-like background.
illustration.alt: A scared bunny hiding behind sunflowers while Yara looks on with concern

pages.text: "Hello, little bunny," Yara said in her softest, gentlest voice. "You don't need to be scared. I'm your friend." But the bunny stayed hidden. It was too frightened to come out.

illustration.description: Yara kneeling down at bunny level, speaking softly with a gentle smile and outstretched hand. The bunny peeks out slightly from behind the sunflower stalks but still looks hesitant. Warm afternoon light filters through the garden. Yara's expression is patient and caring.
illustration.alt: Yara speaking gently to the hiding bunny

pages.text: Yara had an idea! She picked a fresh, orange carrot from her garden. "Are you hungry, little bunny?" she asked. She placed the carrot on the ground and stepped back. The bunny's nose twitched.

illustration.description: Yara holding a bright orange carrot, placing it gently on the ground near the sunflowers. The bunny's nose is visible, twitching with interest. Yara is taking a step back with a hopeful smile. The garden is lush and green with various vegetables visible. Construction paper texture style.
illustration.alt: Yara offering a carrot to the bunny

pages.text: Slowly, very slowly, the bunny hopped out. It was still scared, but it was also hungry. It took a tiny bite of the carrot. Yara sat very still and quiet, feeling happy that the bunny was brave enough to try.

illustration.description: The gray bunny cautiously emerging from behind the sunflowers, taking a small bite of the orange carrot. The bunny still looks nervous but curious. Yara sits cross-legged nearby, smiling patiently and not moving. Peaceful garden setting with dappled sunlight and soft colors.
illustration.alt: The bunny cautiously eating the carrot while Yara sits quietly

pages.text: Soon the bunny finished the whole carrot! It looked up at Yara and wiggled its little nose. Yara giggled softly. "We're friends now," she whispered. The bunny hopped closer, not scared anymore. They were both so happy!

illustration.description: The bunny sitting closer to Yara, both looking happy and comfortable together. The bunny's ears are perked up and it's looking at Yara with friendly eyes. Yara is sitting with her legs crossed, beaming with joy. The garden around them is bright and welcoming with colorful flowers and vegetables. Both characters radiate happiness and friendship.
illustration.alt: Yara and the bunny sitting together as new friends, both looking happy

metadata.illustrationStyle: Bright, cheerful cartoon style with expressive faces. Bold primary colors. Rounded, friendly shapes. Slightly textured backgrounds like construction paper.

questions.question: How did the bunny feel at first?
questions.explanation: The bunny was scared and sad when Yara first found it hiding behind the sunflowers.

questions.question: What did Yara give the bunny?
questions.explanation: Yara picked a fresh, orange carrot from her garden to share with the hungry bunny.

questions.question: How did Yara speak to the bunny?
questions.explanation: Yara used her softest, gentlest voice so she wouldn't scare the bunny even more.

questions.question: How did both Yara and the bunny feel at the end?
questions.explanation: At the end, both Yara and the bunny were happy because they had become friends.

pages.text: Yara found a _____ bunny hiding behind the sunflowers. The bunny looked very scared and _____.
Yara gave the bunny a fresh _____ from her garden. She spoke in a _____ voice so the bunny wouldn't be scared.
